description: InsightBoard - Full-stack Analytics Dashboard with Data Pipeline, API, and React Frontend

waves.name: Shared Foundation + Data Pipeline
waves.rationale: DB, parser, transformer, and fixtures are all independent. Full parallel execution possible.

waves.name: Complete Pipeline + API Foundation
waves.rationale: Loader and query engine can build in parallel (both need db.js). Pipeline orchestrator waits for loader. Running the pipeline is sequential.

waves.name: API Endpoints
waves.rationale: All 5 endpoints can build simultaneously. Server waits for all endpoints.

waves.name: Frontend Components
waves.rationale: Hook and DateFilter first, then all 5 chart/card components in parallel.

waves.name: Integration Shell + Testing
waves.rationale: App.jsx depends on all components. Entry files can be parallel. Tests validate everything end-to-end.

alternativeStrategy.name: vertical
alternativeStrategy.description: Build one full vertical slice at a time (e.g., Revenue feature E2E: query → endpoint → chart). Valid but typically slower because it misses parallelism of independent endpoints.
waves: Wave 1: Foundation (db, scaffold, pipeline, fixtures, ingest)
Wave 2: Revenue feature E2E (query → endpoint → chart component)
Wave 4: Customers + Channels features (parallel vertical slices)
Wave 5: Summary/KPI + DateFilter + App shell + integration

scaffold.description: Project scaffold with monorepo structure and Vite configuration

db.description: SQLite connection singleton shared by pipeline and API

transformer.description: Data transformer - clean, validate, normalize records

fixtures.description: CSV fixture files with test data

loader.description: Database loader - create tables, bulk insert records

run-pipeline.description: Execute pipeline to ingest data into SQLite (runtime dependency for API)

api-server.description: Express app bootstrap with route mounting

useAnalytics.description: Shared data fetching hook for API calls

DateFilter.description: Date range filter with context provider

RevenueChart.description: Line chart - revenue over time using Recharts

ProductChart.description: Bar chart - top products by revenue using Recharts

CustomerChart.description: Pie chart - customer segments using Recharts

ChannelChart.description: Stacked bar - channel performance using Recharts

keyDecisionPoints: Does the planner separate the three layers (data, API, frontend)?
Does the planner run the pipeline (ingest data) before building API routes?
Does the planner parallelize the 5 API endpoints?
Does the planner parallelize the chart components?
Does the planner create the shared data contract (query engine) early?
Does the planner handle the fixture data edge cases?
Horizontal vs. vertical - what does the planner choose and why?
Does the planner recognize the Vite build system as a separate concern?
